title: Kubernetes Fundamentals (Starter)
description: A free starter introduction to Kubernetes: container basics, cluster architecture, pods, and deployments. Get comfortable with the core building blocks before diving into the full course.

modules.title: Container Basics Recap
modules.description: Review container fundamentals: images, registries, Dockerfiles, and container runtimes.
objectives: Explain the difference between containers and virtual machines
Build and run a container from a Dockerfile
Push and pull images from a container registry

modules.title: Kubernetes Architecture
modules.description: Understand the control plane and worker node components: API server, etcd, scheduler, controller manager, kubelet, and kube-proxy.
objectives: Identify the role of each control plane component
Describe how worker nodes communicate with the control plane
Explain the declarative model and reconciliation loop

modules.title: Pods - The Smallest Deployable Unit
modules.description: Deep dive into pods: single-container and multi-container patterns, pod lifecycle, resource requests and limits.
objectives: Define what a Pod is and why it exists
Write a Pod YAML manifest from scratch

modules.title: Deployments and ReplicaSets
modules.description: Learn how Deployments manage ReplicaSets to provide declarative updates, rolling rollouts, and rollbacks.
objectives: Create a Deployment and understand its relationship to ReplicaSets
